mission.title: Install and Configure NATS
mission.description: NATS is a high-performance messaging system designed for cloud-native applications, enabling efficient communication between services and devices. It is suitable for scenarios requiring low-latency messaging and scalability. Key trade-offs include simplicity versus advanced features, as well as the need for additional components for persistence and advanced messaging patterns.

steps.title: Add the NATS Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official NATS Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add nats https://charts.nats.io

```

> This is the official NATS Helm chart repository.

steps.title: Install NATS with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs NATS in the 'nats' namespace using the specified version of the Helm chart.

```bash

kubectl create namespace nats

helm install nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.0

```

> Create a namespace for NATS.
> Install NATS server using Helm.

steps.title: Verify NATS installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the NATS pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Ensure all NATS pods are in 'Running' state.

steps.title: Create a NATS configuration resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for NATS configuration to manage its settings.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

```

> This creates a NATS cluster with 3 replicas.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify NATS custom resource
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the NATS custom resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats

```

> Check the status of the NATS custom resource.

uninstall.title: Uninstall NATS
uninstall.description:
This step removes the NATS installation and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall nats --namespace nats

kubectl delete namespace nats

```

> This will remove the NATS deployment.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade NATS
upgrade.description:
Backup the current configuration, upgrade the NATS installation, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

helm upgrade nats nats/nats --namespace nats --version 2.11.1

kubectl get pods -n nats

```

> Backup the current NATS configuration.
> Upgrade NATS to the new version.
> Verify that the NATS pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nats -n nats -o yaml > nats-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: NATS pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to misconfiguration in the NATS settings.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n nats' to identify configuration issues.

troubleshooting.title: NATS service is not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to network policies or service misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service is correctly configured and check network policies that may be blocking access.

troubleshooting.title: NATS custom resource is not ready
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The NATS cluster may not have enough resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the NATS custom resource definition.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Helm chart version may not exist in the repository.

**Fix:**
Verify the available versions using 'helm search repo nats/nats' and adjust the version accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for JetStream, improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.10, ensure to review JetStream configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the NATS pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n nats' showing 'Running' status.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: nats.io/v1
kind: Nats
metadata:
  name: my-nats
  namespace: nats
spec:
  size: 3
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
